violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Food Safety Managers are not registered with RIDOH. Establishment is to register individual with RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

Pasta salad and tomato salad was observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit on the cold serving line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food Items were voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The condensate from the Pizza prep refrigeration unit was observed frozen on the walls of the unit. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The Ice machine was turned off and emptied at time of inspection. Ice machine is to be cleaned and sanitized. Establishment is to send photos of cleaned and sanitized ice machine to RIDOH before resuming use. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue to work with pest control to eliminate flies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2024
The prep unit lid located in the pizza prep area was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of under the prep sink, stove, and storage area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. NFCS must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2024

The condensate from the from the prep unit refrigeration unit was observed frozen in excess on the unit. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2024
Underneath the hot plate equipment located in the pizza area was not in good repair showing the insulation. Must keep in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

Pizza, which was previously cooked, was only reheated to 138 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Pizza was re-heated to 188 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

Pizzas were observed held at 112-120-124 degrees Fahrenheit at the hot plate unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Proof of registration should be show to inspector at the re-inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2023

Pizzas were observed held between 102-125 degrees Fahrenheit at the hot plate unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. per employee pizzas were hold for less than 1 hour. Re-heated was observed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023

Top ambient temperature of the pizza prep-station unit was observed held at 47-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Bottom Ambient temperature was observed 40 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Top unit was not able to hold 41 degrees F. Non TCS food items should be store on top unit until unit hold temperatures of 41 degrees F or below. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items that exceed the TCS code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the 2 door reach-in freezer and mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023

The floor in the stablishment area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2023
